---
title: "Facets (Subplots)"
format:
  html:
    code-tools: true
jupyter: plotnine-polars
---

This notebook adapts the plotnine guide on facets to the `plotnine_polars`
style.

```{python}
import polars as pl
import plotnine_polars
from plotnine.data import mpg
```

## Setup

```{python}
mpg = pl.from_pandas(mpg)
mpg.head()
```

Here is a single large plot that we can split into subplots.

```{python}
(
    mpg.ggplot()
    .aes("displ", "hwy", color="class")
    .geom_point()
)
```

## `facet_wrap()`: Subplot Sequence

```{python}
(
    mpg.ggplot()
    .aes("displ", "hwy", color="class")
    .geom_point(show_legend=False)
    .facet_wrap("class")
)
```

Use `ncol=` or `nrow=` to control the layout.

```{python}
(
    mpg.ggplot()
    .aes("displ", "hwy", color="class")
    .geom_point(show_legend=False)
    .facet_wrap("class", ncol=2)
)
```

## `facet_grid()`: Subplot Matrix

```{python}
(
    mpg.ggplot()
    .aes("displ", "hwy")
    .geom_point()
    .facet_grid("cyl", "year")
)
```

## Facetting Formula Syntax

Both `facet_wrap()` and `facet_grid()` support a formula-like syntax.

```{python}
(
    mpg.ggplot()
    .aes("displ", "hwy")
    .geom_point()
    .facet_grid("cyl ~ year")
)
```

## `scales=` for Freeing Axes

```{python}
(
    mpg.ggplot()
    .aes("displ", "hwy")
    .geom_point()
    .facet_grid("cyl ~ year", scales="free_y")
)
```
